composition.Programmer 1: Fix and enhance the main backend Python file (main.py) for the OnlineCourse web application. Specifically, address the user session management and user data loading issues that cause the dashboard page to return a 404 error with "User not found". Ensure that the 'data/users.txt' file is properly checked and created with default example user data (including "john|john@student.com|John Student") if missing, to guarantee the session user exists. Maintain all original task requirements including routing for all nine pages, session management, business logic, and file operations on local text files in the 'data' directory. Coordinate with frontend and static asset developers by providing stable backend routes and data structures.
composition.Programmer 2: Develop frontend templates in the 'templates/' directory. Complete all nine HTML template files  dashboard.html, course_catalog.html, course_details.html, my_courses.html, course_learning.html, my_assignments.html, submit_assignment.html, certificates.html, user_profile.html. Ensure all elements have the specified IDs as per the requirements document. Use templating syntax to dynamically render user-specific and course-specific data. All URLs in the frontend code should be routes defined in the backend, not direct HTML file names. Implement page titles, layouts, and UI elements exactly as described in the requirements. Coordinate with the backend developer to align template variables and routes.
composition.Programmer 3: Develop static assets in the 'static/' directory. Create CSS styles in css/styles.css to style the web pages consistently and responsively. Create JavaScript code in js/scripts.js to handle client-side interactivity, UI behavior, button actions, and form validations. Ensure static assets support the functionality and usability of all pages as per the requirements. Coordinate with backend and frontend developers to ensure UI elements and interactions are fully supported.
